response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced polyglot engineer with a strong distributed systems and software craftsmanship background, but he fails the most critical hard requirement for this role: 8 years of C++ experience. His resume lists approximately 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015), with C++ absent entirely from his skills section. His primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Additionally, while he has touched AWS CDK and Kubernetes, there is no evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform at the scale or depth this role demands, and his technical leadership has been in web/product engineering rather than infrastructure or DevOps mentorship.
strengths: Demonstrated distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, high-throughput architecture
Consistent evidence of software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and community mentoring through workshops and talks
Staff-level tenure at reputable companies (Stripe, Shopify) showing product scale and cross-functional collaboration
concerns: C++ experience is ~6 months versus the 8-year minimum requirement — this is a disqualifying gap for the role's core technical requirement
No demonstrated ownership of a cloud compute platform or internal infrastructure product; cloud exposure is limited to AWS CDK usage as a consumer
No evidence of 2+ years leading or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers; mentoring has been through community workshops rather than engineering org leadership
